_meta.description: Structured knowledge base for the Diego AI Sales Concierge. Used as context injection for search_knowledge_base tool calls and potential future RAG integration.

brand.name: The Mambo Guild
brand.tagline: Autonomy in practice. Science in technique. Serving over perfection.
brand.style: Salsa On2 (New York Style Mambo) — strictly. One style, done deeply.
brand.founderRole: Lead instructor, certified Learning Experience Designer (LXD), personal coach for Guild Master members.
coreBeliefs: The person who makes the most mistakes, most publicly, improves the fastest.
Motor learning science — not vibes — should drive curriculum design.
Dance belongs to everyone willing to work for it.

trial.access: Full platform access — identical to Guild Member tier
trial.cancelPolicy: Cancel anytime during or after trial. No commitment.

tiers.name: Guild Member
features: The Lab — technique Q&A (10 question slots)
The Stage — video feedback community (5 video slots)

tiers.name: Guild Master
tiers.spotsNote: Capped at 30 active members. When full, a waitlist applies.
features: Monthly 1-on-1 video feedback from Pavle (submit 1 video per month, max 100 seconds)
Private Guild Master Zoom — The Roundtable (weekly live calls with Pavle)
The Vault — full archive of all past Roundtable recordings
DJ Booth (Mambo Mixer) — isolated stems: percussion, piano/bass, vocals/brass, full mix
Exclusive Guild Master badge on public profile

courseTypes.description: Full structured learning paths with weeks and days
Standalone choreographies — full routines to learn and perform
Focused deep-dives: training science, mambo history, musicality

lessonTypes.description: Core video instruction with notes and optional quiz

bossBattle.description: Culminating challenge for each module tier. Student records themselves performing the technique and submits the video for instructor review.
bossBattle.reward: Unlocks the next Skill Tree tier

notableTopics: Mambo History — based on Juliet McMains' 'Spinning Mambo into Salsa' (20 modules)
Training Science — cognitive science of motor learning, deliberate practice (18 modules)

skillTree.name: The Constellation
skillTree.visualization: Interactive star-map — animated gold edges between prerequisite nodes, hover GIF previews, locked/unlocked states
skillTree.progression: Each tier requires completing the module's lessons AND passing the Boss Battle video submission
skillTree.xpSystem: Each lesson awards XP (default 50 XP). XP accumulates to level up your profile.

families.name: Firestarter
families.trigger: Receiving 🔥 Fire reactions on Stage videos

families.name: The Professor
families.trigger: Getting accepted answers in The Lab

families.name: Founder
families.trigger: Waitlist / beta tester status; also 3+ referrals

clavesEconomy.name: Claves
clavesEconomy.namingNote: Named after the clave rhythm instrument — the heartbeat of salsa music.
clavesEconomy.philosophy: Earned through courage. Spent on community participation. The system rewards making mistakes openly.

spending.action: Post a question in The Lab
Post a video to The Stage

theStage.model: Instagram-style video sharing for dance progress
features: WIP (Work In Progress) banner for honest rough submissions

tools.name: A/B Loop
tools.description: Set a start and end point on the video timeline. The player loops that section automatically — drill a 3-second movement segment on infinite repeat without touching the scrubber.

tools.name: Frame-by-Frame
tools.description: Step through video one frame at a time. Essential for analysing foot placement, hip movement, or arm styling at sub-second resolution.

tools.name: Speed Control
tools.description: Playback from 0.25x to 2x speed, with pitch preservation. Watch a fast spin at quarter speed to catch what's actually happening in the body.

streakSystem.mechanic: Increment on daily login. Reset if a day is missed (unless a freeze is used).
streakSystem.badgeFamily: Metronome / Unstoppable — Bronze at 7 days, Silver at 30, Gold at 100, Diamond at 365

theRoundtable.description: Weekly live Zoom calls hosted by Pavle for Guild Master members.
features: Live Q&A on technique, training, music
theRoundtable.archiving: All sessions recorded and stored in The Vault

coaching.description: Monthly personal video feedback from Pavle.
howItWorks: Student uploads dance video through the platform
Optional: include one specific question (140 characters)
Optional: consent to use clip for social media (+50 XP if yes)
Pavle reviews and records a video feedback response
Student receives email notification when feedback is ready
Watch feedback via secure link in the Guild Master hub

djBooth.description: Real salsa tracks with professionally separated audio stems.
djBooth.useCase: Practice dancing to just the clave and congas to develop rhythmic independence. Or isolate the piano guajeo to train musicality.

theVault.description: Archive of all past Roundtable recording sessions.

faqs.question: Is this for complete beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Skill Tree starts at ground zero — weight transfer, timing basics, the clave. You don't need any prior dance experience. The system is designed to give you a clear path whether you've never danced or you've been dancing for years.

faqs.question: Do I need a partner to benefit from this?
faqs.answer: No. The majority of the curriculum is solo — footwork, body mechanics, styling, musicality. Having a partner helps you apply what you learn, but it's not a prerequisite to getting value from the platform.

faqs.question: Why On2 specifically? Why not On1?
faqs.answer: On2 (New York Style Mambo) is the style with the richest musical relationship to the clave — the heartbeat of Afro-Cuban music. It's where the music and movement align most truthfully. We chose depth over breadth. One style, done right.

faqs.question: Can I cancel anytime?
faqs.answer: Yes. No contracts, no cancellation fees. Cancel from your profile or subscription settings page. If you cancel during the $1 trial you won't be charged anything.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Guild Member and Guild Master?
faqs.answer: Guild Member gives you the full curriculum, skill tree, community, and pro tools. Guild Master adds the monthly 1-on-1 video coaching with Pavle, access to weekly live Zoom calls (The Roundtable), the full archive (The Vault), and the DJ Booth stem isolation tool. It's capped at 30 spots to maintain the quality of the personal coaching.

faqs.question: How does the $1 trial work?
faqs.answer: You get full Guild Member access for 7 days for $1. After 7 days, your card is charged $39/month unless you cancel. There's no locked-in period — cancel anytime before the 7 days are up and you pay only $1 total.

faqs.question: What are Claves (🥢)?
faqs.answer: Claves are the in-platform currency, named after the percussion instrument at the heart of salsa music. You earn them by showing up daily, posting your dancing, helping others in The Lab, and maintaining streaks. You spend them to participate in the community. The system is designed so that courage — putting your imperfect dancing out there — is rewarded.

faqs.question: How is this different from YouTube tutorials?
faqs.answer: Three things: structure, accountability, and community. YouTube gives you fragments. The Guild gives you a system — a 38-tier path with clear prerequisites, Boss Battle checkpoints, and a community that gives you real feedback. The A/B Loop and frame-by-frame tools alone are worth more than any random tutorial.

faqs.question: I've been dancing for 3 years. Is this too basic for me?
faqs.answer: Almost certainly not. The Skill Tree has 38 tiers. Most self-taught dancers with 3 years of experience have significant gaps in body mechanics, musicality, or both — they just don't know what they don't know. The Lab and Boss Battle system are particularly valuable for intermediate dancers.

faqs.question: Who is Pavle Popovic?
faqs.answer: Pavle is the founder and lead instructor of The Mambo Guild. He's a certified Learning Experience Designer (LXD) with deep expertise in dance pedagogy and training science. Guild Master members work with him directly through the monthly 1-on-1 coaching.

objectionHandling.response: In-person learning is irreplaceable for partner dynamics — full stop. But motor learning science is clear: the volume of repetitions, the ability to slow technique to 25% speed and loop it 40 times, the structured feedback loop — video instruction wins in those dimensions. The Guild is built to complement in-person lessons, not replace them. Most members use both.

$39/month is $1.30 a day. One in-person group class is $20–30. The Guild gives you 40 hours of structured content, daily — with tools, community, and a skill map. The $1 trial costs less than a cup of coffee and gives you 7 days to decide for yourself.
Lessons are modular — 5 to 20 minutes each. You don't need an hour. You need a lunch break and an A/B loop. The streak system is designed for real life — with one free weekly freeze built in.

That feeling is the point. The platform was built for people who feel not ready. The Skill Tree shows you exactly where to start. The Boss Battle tells you when you're actually ready to move forward — not when you think you look good enough.

objectionHandling.objection: I tried online learning and it didn't work
objectionHandling.response: Was it structured? Did it tell you where you stood, what to work on next, and give you community accountability? Most online dance content is a pile of fragments without a roadmap. That's the problem The Guild was built to solve.
